UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user account on the 'धनधारा' platform, including their profile information, social connections, and monetization status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The display name of the user.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login (e.g., Google Login).

phone.description: The user's phone number, used for login (e.g., Mobile OTP Login).

profilePhotoUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

bio.description: A short biographical description provided by the user.

followerIds.description: An array of UserProfile IDs that follow this user. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

followingIds.description: An array of UserProfile IDs that this user is following. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

followerCount.description: The total number of followers this user has. (Denormalized count)

monetizationEnabled.description: Indicates if the user has met the criteria (e.g., 1000 followers) and is enabled for monetization.

isAdmin.description: Flag indicating if this user has administrative privileges.

isBlocked.description: Flag indicating if this user has been blocked by an administrator.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Post.title: Post
Post.description: Represents a photo post made by a user on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Post entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this post. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Post)

photoUrl.description: URL to the photo content of the post.

description.description: A detailed description or caption for the post.

likeIds.description: An array of UserProfile IDs who have liked this post. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Post)

commentIds.description: An array of Comment IDs associated with this post. (Relationship: Post 1:N Comment)

isFeatured.description: Indicates if the post has been set as a 'Top Featured Post' by an administrator.

isModerated.description: Flag indicating if the post has undergone content moderation (e.g., by AI or admin).

moderationStatus.description: The current moderation status of the post (e.g., 'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'flagged').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the post was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the post was last updated.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a comment made by a user on a specific post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

postId.description: Reference to the Post this comment belongs to. (Relationship: Post 1:N Comment)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made this comment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Comment)

content.description: The text content of the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was last updated.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification sent to a user about social interactions or system events.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

recipientId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who is the recipient of this notification. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Notification)

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who triggered this notification (e.g., followed, liked, commented). Optional for system notifications. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Notification)

relatedPostId.description: Reference to the Post entity related to this notification (if applicable). (Relationship: Post 1:N Notification)

relatedCommentId.description: Reference to the Comment entity related to this notification (if applicable). (Relationship: Comment 1:N Notification)

isRead.description: Indicates if the recipient has viewed this notification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was created.

AppSetting.title: AppSetting
AppSetting.description: A singleton entity to store global application settings and configurations, managed by an administrator.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AppSetting entity. This should be a fixed value like 'global_settings' as it's a singleton.

adsenseCodeHtml.description: The HTML code provided by Google AdSense to be embedded in the website for displaying ads.

featuredPostId.description: Reference to the ID of the Post currently set as 'Top Featured' on the homepage. (Relationship: Post 1:1 AppSetting)

privacyPolicyContent.description: The full content of the privacy policy, typically in HTML or Markdown format, displayed on the legal page.

termsAndConditionsContent.description: The full content of the terms and conditions, typically in HTML or Markdown format, displayed on the legal page.

disclaimerContent.description: The full content of the disclaimer, typically in HTML or Markdown format, displayed on the legal page.

contactEmail.description: The official email address for contact inquiries.

contactPhone.description: The official phone number for contact inquiries.

aboutUsContent.description: The content for the 'About Us' page, describing the platform's purpose and system.

aiModerationEnabled.description: A flag to enable or disable the AI-powered content moderation tool globally.

minFollowersForMonetization.description: The minimum number of followers a user needs to activate monetization.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the application settings were last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. The document ID '{userId}' must match 'request.auth.uid' for writes and also serve as the 'ownerId'. Publicly readable for profile pages, writable only by the owning user. Includes denormalized 'followerCount', 'monetizationEnabled', 'isAdmin', 'isBlocked' for independent authorization and UI display.

A collection used for database access control (DBAC). Each document represents an administrator, where the document ID '{adminId}' is the user's UID. The documents themselves can be minimal (e.g., just an 'id' field matching the document ID). This structure supports authorization independence, allowing rules to check 'request.auth.uid' against this collection to grant admin privileges without custom claims or 'get()' calls.

Stores all photo posts made by users. The 'userId' field within the document indicates the owner. Posts are publicly readable (subject to 'moderationStatus'). Writable/editable by the owner ('userId') or an administrator. Includes denormalized 'userId' (ownerId) for authorization independence, 'moderationStatus' for content visibility, and 'isFeatured' for homepage display. Photos should not be directly downloadable via 'photoUrl' but shared via post links, controlled by Firebase Storage rules.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier for the post.

definition.description: Stores comments for a specific post. Writable only by the 'userId' who created the comment or an administrator. Readable by anyone who can read the parent post. Includes denormalized 'userId' (ownerId) and 'postId' for authorization independence.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent post.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the comment.

definition.description: Represents a user's 'like' on a post. The existence of a document with ID '{userId}' signifies that this user has liked the '{postId}'. Documents can be minimal (e.g., just an 'id' or 'createdAt' field). Writable/deletable only by the '{userId}' represented by the document ID. Readable to check who liked a post. This allows for scalable like counts and efficient rule checks without large arrays, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the post that was liked.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who liked the post.

definition.description: Records that '{followerUserId}' is following '{followedUserId}'. The existence of a document at this path signifies the follow relationship. Documents can be minimal. Writable/deletable only by the '{followerUserId}' to establish or remove the follow. This enables scalable follower/following lists and efficient rule checks for authorization independence.

params.name: followedUserId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user being followed.

params.name: followerUserId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is following.

definition.description: Stores personal notifications for a user. Owned and readable/writable only by the '{recipientId}'. The 'recipientId' from the path provides authorization independence. Includes relevant context fields like 'senderId', 'type', and 'isRead'.

params.name: recipientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is the recipient of the notification.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification.

definition.description: A singleton document containing global application settings and configurations. Only writable by administrators. This includes fields like 'featuredPostId', 'adsenseCodeHtml', 'privacyPolicyContent', and 'minFollowersForMonetization'. Its fixed path ensures easy access and clear security rules.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for 'धनधारा' is designed prioritizing Authorization Independence and secure Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) as core principles. This ensures robust security rules that are simple to write and debug, and a scalable backend.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** User data is stored directly at `/users/{request.auth.uid}`. The `userId` in the path directly corresponds to the authenticated user's ID, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to verify ownership for profile reads/writes.
2.  **Admin Roles (`/admins/{adminId}`):** Instead of relying on a flag within the user profile (which would require a `get()` call), a separate top-level collection `/admins/{adminId}` is used. An admin's `uid` exists as a document ID in this collection. Security rules can check for the existence of `request.auth.uid` in this collection, fulfilling the 'Existence over Content' DBAC mandate and ensuring authorization independence for admin actions.
3.  **Posts (`/posts/{postId}`):** Each post document includes a `userId` field, explicitly indicating the owner. This denormalized `userId` allows security rules to instantly verify ownership (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`) without fetching any parent document, enabling atomic post creation and updates.
4.  **Comments (`/posts/{postId}/comments/{commentId}`):** Similar to posts, each comment document contains its `userId` (owner) and `postId`. The `postId` is also available from the path. This denormalization means all necessary authorization context for a comment is either within the document or in its path, avoiding `get()` calls.
5.  **Likes (`/posts/{postId}/likes/{userId}`):** The act of liking a post is represented by the existence of a document where the ID `{userId}` is the `uid` of the user who liked the post. The `postId` is available from the path. This structure inherently provides all context for authorization (`request.auth.uid == userId`) and eliminates `get()`.
6.  **Follower/Following Relationships (`/users/{followedUserId}/followers/{followerUserId}`):** Follower relationships are established by creating a document at a specific path where both `followedUserId` and `followerUserId` are directly available in the path. This directly links the `request.auth.uid` to the action of following/unfollowing, ensuring authorization independence.
7.  **Notifications (`/users/{recipientId}/notifications/{notificationId}`):** Notifications are strictly owned by the recipient. The `recipientId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`, making authorization straightforward and independent.

**Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs):**
1.  **Structural Segregation:** Data is segregated into collections with homogeneous security postures. For example, all documents in `/posts` have similar public read/owner-write security needs. Private user data (e.g., notifications) resides in user-specific subcollections (`/users/{userId}/notifications`). Global settings are in a dedicated singleton path (`/app_settings/global_settings`) with admin-only write access. This prevents mixing different security requirements within a single collection, simplifying security rules and making `list` operations secure.
2.  **Explicit Ownership and Status Fields:** Fields like `Post.userId`, `Comment.userId`, and `Post.moderationStatus` are explicitly used in documents. This allows security rules to apply fine-grained controls during `list` operations (e.g., only allowing reads for posts with `moderationStatus: 'approved'`).
3.  **Existence-Based Authorization for Relationships:** Collections like `/posts/{postId}/likes/{userId}` and `/users/{followedUserId}/followers/{followerUserId}` are designed for efficient querying of many-to-many relationships. Listing all followers of a user or all likes for a post is efficient, and rules can directly check document existence for authorization without filtering large arrays client-side. This ensures that only authorized data is returned, adhering to QAPs.
